name: Agent definition discovery and precedence
description: Agent definitions are markdown files with YAML frontmatter discovered from package agents/, user ~/.pi/agent/agents, and project .pi/agents. discoverDefinitions loads package first, then user, then project into a Map keyed by name, so project definitions override user/package definitions and user overrides package. Frontmatter can include model, thinking, tools, skills, skillTemplates, and extensionTemplates.

title: Orchestrator is a root session role, not a spawnable agent type
rationale: The interactive Pi shell becomes the orchestrator when /orchestrate is enabled; child RPC agents launched with --skill/--extension should be limited to spawnable operational classes such as lead, scout, implementer, and reviewer. Orchestrator-only skills/templates/extensions from Orchestrator Libraries should be guarded from child agent resolution rather than exposed as normal spawnable agent capabilities.

title: Agent definitions now use spawnable classes
rationale: Agent definitions support a frontmatter `class` parsed into agentClass. The spawnable classes are lead, scout, implementer, and reviewer; orchestrator is reserved for the interactive /orchestrate root session. Dashboard save flows only offer spawnable classes, API/tool/server spawn paths reject orchestrator-class definitions, and the package pio-example-orchestrator is marked as a non-spawnable root profile reference.

evidence.file: extensions/multi-agent/definitions.ts; extensions/multi-agent/index.ts; extensions/multi-agent/server.ts; extensions/multi-agent/spawn.ts; web/app.tsx; agents/pio-example-orchestrator.md

title: Root orchestrator profiles are separate from spawnable agent definitions
rationale: The root `/orchestrate` session now activates markdown root profiles discovered from package defaults, user/project profile directories, and Orchestrator Libraries. Profiles contribute root-only instructions and orchestrator-eligible skills/skill templates via `resources_discover` and `before_agent_start`, while spawned child agents still use agent definitions and extension templates. A built-in `default` profile is always shipped so plain `/orchestrate` has a fallback unless multiple profiles require user selection.
